summary: Successfully implemented the complete ContentPreprocessor pipeline for YCB-style content ingestion. Created a 4-stage pipeline (Extract → Clean → Enrich → Store) with comprehensive entity extraction, content classification, and metadata enrichment. All CLI integrations are working, and the existing content_ingest.py has been refactored to use the new pipeline. Pipeline handles HTML (trafilatura), PDF (pdfplumber), and Markdown files with robust error handling and dry-run support.
learnings: Entity extraction using simple heuristics is surprisingly effective - captured emails, URLs, money amounts, dates, and capitalized phrases (people/companies) without heavy NLP dependencies
Pipeline design with individual stage execution provides excellent debugging and testing capabilities - can isolate issues to specific stages
Trafilatura HTML extraction works best with temporary files rather than direct string processing - had to create temp file approach
Content classification (article vs link vs social) should happen after extraction but before enrichment for optimal metadata generation
Backward compatibility with existing content_ingest.py was achieved through optional --use-pipeline flag rather than breaking changes
CLI integration pattern of delegating to existing main functions through sys.argv reconstruction works well for maintaining separate tool interfaces
